meta_title: For Freelancers: portfolio building to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Portfolio Building to Scale SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Use this intermediate checklist to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build a high-converting portfolio that attracts clients.
content.answer_block: Break the cycle of basic CRUD apps by leveraging AI-driven architectural patterns. Use Antigravity to scaffold complex, modular systems and Claude Code to automate implementation. By focusing on high-impact, real-world utility in your portfolio, you shift from being a 'coder' to a 'solutions architect' that clients pay a premium to hire.
content.intro:
You’re stuck in the endless loop of Todo lists and weather apps. Every project feels like a carbon copy of the last, and your portfolio is screaming 'junior' to every high-paying client who lands on your site. You have the technical skills, but your output lacks the architectural depth that separates a freelancer from a consultant.

It’s time to pivot. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for surgical code implementation, you can transform a single weekend into a production-grade SaaS case study. This isn't just about building; it's about showcasing the complex, scalable systems that solve actual business problems.

steps.title: Define the Business-Centric Blueprint
steps.description: Stop building features and start building solutions. Identify a specific industry pain point and map the architecture before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level technical architecture document for a multi-tenant SaaS application that solves [Insert Problem]. Include database schema, API structure, and a scalable authentication flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a README.md outlining the system architecture and core business logic for this project.'

steps.title: Scaffold with Antigravity Modules
steps.description: Use modular scaffolding to ensure your project is clean, maintainable, and impressive to technical leads evaluating your work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular boilerplate using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Implement a clean folder structure separating services, components, and hooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure based on the Antigravity modular boilerplate guidelines.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to rapidly build the core business logic, ensuring it adheres to dry, clean-code principles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this logic into a reusable service class that handles data validation and error logging consistently across the application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core business logic for the data processing service, ensuring robust error handling.'

steps.title: Integrate High-Value Third-Party APIs
steps.description: Clients love integrations. Use Claude Code to wire up complex APIs like Stripe, OpenAI, or Twilio to demonstrate real-world experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an abstraction layer for a Stripe payment integration that handles webhooks and user subscription states effectively.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Set up the Stripe integration layer and secure webhook handler endpoints.'

steps.title: Audit and Optimize for Performance
steps.description: Show you care about scale. Use AI to audit your code for performance bottlenecks and security vulnerabilities before the final deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a senior-level code review of this project. Identify potential security flaws, performance bottlenecks, and areas for optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scan the codebase for security vulnerabilities and suggest performance optimizations for React rendering.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Design Decisions' document in your repo; clients value the 'why' more than the 'how'.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for 80% of your business logic; it proves you ship production-ready code.
Build a 'Client Dashboard' view even if the app is a tool; it demonstrates you understand the business stakeholder's perspective.
Keep your commit history clean; use Claude Code to squash and describe commits in a professional, chronological format.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a high-value problem rather than five mediocre features.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, update your portfolio site with a Loom video walkthrough of the app, and start reaching out to potential clients showcasing the specific problem you solved.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop settling for basic work and start building high-value assets. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
